summary: Cathy O'Neil announces that her algorithmic auditing company ORCAA was featured in MIT Technology Review's 'Download' section on emerging technology. The brief post celebrates recent media coverage, noting that both the MIT Technology Review piece and a Wired article from the same week have generated business inquiries for ORCAA. O'Neil expresses optimism about the growing interest in algorithmic accountability services.
key_points: ORCAA was featured in MIT Technology Review's emerging technology section
The company audits algorithms for bias
Recent media coverage (MIT Tech Review and Wired) has generated business inquiries
There is growing mainstream interest in algorithmic accountability
money_quotes: This and the Wired piece last week have led to a bunch of inquiries for ORCAA, which is super exciting!
key_insight: Algorithmic auditing is gaining recognition as an important emerging field, with mainstream tech publications now covering companies that hold algorithms accountable for bias.
